key_insight: The paper shows that two-dimensional polycrystalline disk packings can be made both stealthy and hyperuniform by introducing long-range grain correlations, unlike ordinary polycrystals.
engineering_relevance: These SHU microstructures could enable transparent, low-scattering metamaterials and improved transport media for photonics and diffusion-based applications.
method: Generate 2D identical-disk packings using collective-coordinate optimization to enforce stealthy hyperuniform order.
Compare against modified Lubachevsky–Stillinger rapid-compression polycrystals as a realistic nonhyperuniform baseline.
Evaluate structure and function via grain statistics, surface area, spreadability, and dielectric response.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports extensive comparisons showing distinct grain-size distributions and that SHU packings become perfectly transparent at small wave vectors while LS packings do not.
watch_item: These microstructures are not expected to form spontaneously, so practical use depends on precise fabrication control such as nanolithography or 3D printing.
paper_title: Two-dimensional stealthy hyperuniform polycrystalline disk packings
